history: The patient has a longstanding pattern of difficult interactions with their mother, marked by strained communication and delays in sharing important family events. This history has contributed to the patient feeling disconnected and possibly unsupported within their family system. The patient may have developed a tendency to anticipate negative outcomes in family interactions, as suggested by their emotional and behavioral responses to upcoming visits.
intermediate_belief: If I am not careful in how I interact with my parents, especially my mother, things will go badly and I will end up feeling hurt or misunderstood. I have to manage the situation to avoid conflict or disappointment. If I let my guard down, I will be emotionally exposed.
intermediate_belief_depression: No matter what I do, my efforts to connect with my family won't make a difference, so there's no point in trying to change things.
coping_strategies: The patient tends to avoid direct confrontation and may rehearse conversations or plan activities to keep interactions superficial. They may seek reassurance from others and distract themselves with work or hobbies to manage anxiety about the visit. Emotional withdrawal and procrastination on visit preparations are also used to cope with anticipated distress.
situation: Preparing for an upcoming visit from parents.
auto_thought: This visit is going to be tense and uncomfortable. No matter what I do, something will go wrong and I'll end up feeling upset.
behavior: I avoid bringing up important topics and stick to safe conversations. I rehearse what I might say to prevent conflict. I procrastinate on getting ready for the visit and distract myself with other activities.
